title: Project Telos Eighteenth-Wave Non-GitHub Outreach Claims

claims.text: The eighteenth-wave packet shifts current outreach focus away from GitHub and toward funding, Reddit, YouTube, forums, and broader developer communities unless an active maintainer/CI response requires GitHub attention.
claims.falsification: The packet continues prioritizing new GitHub outreach, omits the non-GitHub shift, or lacks a GitHub exception boundary.

claims.text: The packet records current Telos tool evidence including Gather 1.5.0 MATCH, Index 2.8.0 MATCH, Forum 1.12.0 MATCH, Crucible 1.1.0 MATCH, Telos room 5 of 5 ready, and 70 available protocol tools.
claims.falsification: The packet omits current tool evidence or records tool versions/statuses inconsistent with the current MCP outputs.

claims.text: The packet treats Reddit and YouTube comments as ready-to-confirm external side effects, not as already submitted posts.
claims.falsification: The packet claims comments were posted, instructs blind automation without action-time confirmation, or omits exact destination/text confirmation requirements.

claims.text: The funding section updates active paths with current public-source constraints: OpenAI Cybersecurity Grant/Trusted Access is high-readiness defensive funding; Patch the Planet is a maintainer-support positioning path; NLnet is temporarily narrowed to Taler/Fediversity while CodeSupply is prepare-only; FLOSS/fund requires a public funding manifest and has license/maturity blockers; MOSS is parked as inactive.
claims.falsification: The packet treats inactive or blocked programs as ready-to-submit, omits core blockers, or claims official partnerships/funding.

claims.text: The first social submission set includes eight exact comments across Reddit and YouTube, with no more than one link-bearing comment in the first batch.
claims.falsification: The packet lacks exact text/URLs for the first set or includes multiple link-heavy first-batch comments.
